title: Real World Table Tennis

affiliations.name: J. Crayton Pruitt Family Department of Biomedical Engineering, University of Florida, Gainesville, FL 32611, USA
J. Crayton Pruitt Family Department of Biomedical Engineering, University of Florida, Gainesville, FL 32611, USA

description: This dataset comprises multimodal neurophysiological and kinematic recordings from 25 participants engaged in real-world table tennis gameplay. Data include high-density dual-layer electroencephalography, neck electromyography, inertial measurement unit acceleration, structural MRI, and synchronized video recordings. Participants completed 60 minutes of table tennis play against both a ball machine and human opponent, with 10 minutes of standing baseline. Processed data include independent component analysis decomposition and dipole modeling for EEG signals.
methods_description: Data were collected from 25 participants performing real-world table tennis tasks (60 minutes total play with ball machine and human player, plus 10 minutes standing baseline). Recordings included high-density dual-layer EEG, neck EMG, IMU acceleration data, T1-weighted structural MRI, and synchronized video. EEG data underwent independent component analysis decomposition and dipole modeling. Raw data are available in the sourcedata folder.
